Search results for 'Notion pricing plans 2024' (via duckduckgo):
1. Notion Pricing Plans: Free, Plus, Business, & Enterprise.
   https://www.notion.com/pricing
   Learn more about our different pricing plans. We support everyone, from free personal accounts to enterprise businesses.

   https://www.forbes.com/advisor/business/notion-pricing/
   Notion offers straightforward pricing and plans with a free forever plan and premium plans starting at $10 per user per month. Learn more in our pricing guide.

   https://www.notion.com/help/category/plans-billing-and-payment

   https://www.simple.ink/notion-pricing
   Notion pricing starting at $10/month, plus $8 for AI Assistant, it is worth thinking about this decision as sticking to the very generous free plan might also be enough.
6. Notion Pricing (2025): Plans and Feature Overview - Plaky Learn
   https://plaky.com/learn/plaky/notion-pricing/
   Notion’s pricing changes in 2025 Notion changed its prices in mid-2024 and again in May 2025, leaving users confused and losing trust. The main concern Notion’s users have shared is that the feature sets included in each plan will reshuffle again, and that they will lose access to their data.

   Yes. Notion offers a limited free trial with approximately 20 AI responses for new users. This allows you to test features before committing to the monthly or annual subscription. Does pricing change with team size? The per-member rate remains fixed at $10/month (or $8/month annually) regardless of 
8. Notion Pricing 2026: Plans, AI Features & Real Costs Breakdown
   https://userjot.com/blog/notion-pricing-2025-plans-ai-costs-explained
   Notion's AI is now built into higher tiers, not a separate add-on. Compare all plans, guest limits, and real costs. Plus why teams pair Notion with UserJot for the perfect internal + external setup.

data.summary: Fetched https://www.notion.com/pricing but it does not help with this sub-question: The page text shows feature comparisons across pricing tiers (Free, Plus, Business, Enterprise) but does not include the actual dollar prices for any paid plans. The page mentions 'Price in USD' as a header but the specific costs are missing from the provided text. Only add-on pricing is visible: Notion AI credits cost $10 per 1,000 monthly credits, and Workers/custom agents require Notion credits.

extract.summary: The page text shows feature comparisons across pricing tiers (Free, Plus, Business, Enterprise) but does not include the actual dollar prices for any paid plans. key_facts: Free plan available with limited features for multi-member workspaces
Plus, Business, and Enterprise tiers exist but specific USD prices not shown in text
Notion AI costs $10 per 1,000 monthly Notion credits (after free trial)
Pricing is charged per member/paid seat, not per workspace
Students get Plus Plan free with school email
Enterprise tier has custom pricing (contact sales)

data.summary:
Read https://blog.getmanifest.ai/notion-alternatives/:
The page identifies seven top Notion competitors in 2024: ClickUp (versatile project management with task management, document collaboration, and time tracking starting at $5/user/month); Evernote (note-taking tool with multimedia support and web clipping at $7.99/month); Coda (interactive documents with embedded tables and automation at $10/user/month); SmartTask (task management with Gantt charts at $8/user/month); Airtable (flexible database tool with multiple views at $10/user/month); Scrintal (visual knowledge management and mind mapping at $12/user/month); and Nuclino (collaborative documentation with real-time editing at $5/user/month). Each tool targets different user needs—from visual thinkers to teams needing robust collaboration.
Key facts:
  - ClickUp: 'Highly customizable' with comprehensive features; $5/user/month; con: 'steeper learning curve'
  - Evernote: 'Excellent for personal note-taking' with 'powerful search functionality'; $7.99/month
  - Airtable: 'Highly flexible and customizable' database tool with multiple views (grid, calendar, kanban, gallery); $10/user/month
  - Scrintal: Visual knowledge management 'great for visual thinkers' with mind mapping; $12/user/month
  - Nuclino: 'Simple and easy to use' with real-time collaborative editing and knowledge base linking; $5/user/month
  - Coda: 'Versatile' with 'strong integration capabilities' and document automation; $10/user/month

extract.summary: The page identifies seven top Notion competitors in 2024: ClickUp (versatile project management with task management, document collaboration, and time tracking starting at $5/user/month); Evernote (note-taking tool with multimedia support and web clipping at $7.99/month); Coda (interactive documents with embedded tables and automation at $10/user/month); SmartTask (task management with Gantt charts at $8/user/month); Airtable (flexible database tool with multiple views at $10/user/month); Scrintal (visual knowledge management and mind mapping at $12/user/month); and Nuclino (collaborative documentation with real-time editing at $5/user/month). Each tool targets different user needs—from visual thinkers to teams needing robust collaboration.
key_facts: ClickUp: 'Highly customizable' with comprehensive features; $5/user/month; con: 'steeper learning curve'
Evernote: 'Excellent for personal note-taking' with 'powerful search functionality'; $7.99/month
Airtable: 'Highly flexible and customizable' database tool with multiple views (grid, calendar, kanban, gallery); $10/user/month
Scrintal: Visual knowledge management 'great for visual thinkers' with mind mapping; $12/user/month
Nuclino: 'Simple and easy to use' with real-time collaborative editing and knowledge base linking; $5/user/month
Coda: 'Versatile' with 'strong integration capabilities' and document automation; $10/user/month

data.evaluation: Good data on competitors. The most commonly cited top competitors across multiple sources are **Confluence**, **ClickUp**, and **Coda** (or Airtable). Let me now get detailed, current pricing for the top 3 most frequently cited: **Confluence**, **ClickUp**, and **Coda** — and also nail down Notion's exact pricing.

   The Premium Plan (Monthly): At $11.55 per user per month, this tier is designed for teams that need advanced project management features and unlimited storage. The Enterprise Plan: Tailored for large organizations, this plan comes with custom ...

   https://www.atlassian.com/software/confluence/pricing
   We also offer annual subscriptions for Confluence. For these subscriptions, we'll bill you for the tier most closely matching your user count. Depending on the number of users purchased, annual subscriptions may be a better value. If you're unsatisfied with any Atlassian Cloud product or service, we
3. How Much Does Confluence Cost in 2024? Is It Really Free? - Financesonline.com
   https://financesonline.com/how-much-does-confluence-cost/
   July 6, 2025 - The Confluence free plan allows for up to 10 users and 2GB file storage. However, if you have a bigger team, then you may need to upgrade to Standard or Premium plans. For these paid plans, you can expect to pay $5 or $10 per user per month.
4. Confluence Pricing 2026: Plans, Costs, and Real-W...
   https://www.featurebase.app/blog/confluence-pricing
   March 6, 2026 - Confluence pricing plans (Free, Standard, Premium, Enterprise) are priced per user/month and are mostly billed annually.

   https://massivegrid.com/blog/confluence-per-user-pricing-true-cost/

   https://cpoclub.com/tools/confluence-pricing/

   October 22, 2024 - However, Confluence offers more value for money because of its features for analytics and software integrations with other Atlassian products. The pricing ($6.30 to $12.30 per user per month) will offer access to most of Confluence’s features.

data.subquestion: What are Notion's exact pricing tiers and costs in 2024/2025?
data.rationale: Plaky has a detailed Notion pricing overview and was recently updated.

data.summary:
Read https://plaky.com/learn/plaky/notion-pricing/:
Notion has 4 pricing tiers as of 2024/2025: Free ($0), Plus ($10–12/month), Business ($20–24/month), and Enterprise (POA). In May 2025, Notion raised prices and removed the AI add-on, with changes effective August 13, 2025. The Business plan previously cost $18/month but now costs $24/month; Plus remained at $12/month. Notion AI features are now included only in Business and Enterprise plans, no longer available as a separate add-on.
Key facts:
  - Free: $0/month; Plus: $10/user/month (yearly) or $12/user/month (monthly); Business: $20/user/month (yearly) or $24/user/month (monthly); Enterprise: POA
  - May 2025 pricing changes effective August 13, 2025: Plus and Business plan prices increased; feature set restructured; AI add-on removed
  - Business plan price increased from $18/month to $24/month (monthly billing)
  - Notion AI features now included only in Business and Enterprise plans; new Plus users have limited trial access only
  - Previous Business plan with old AI add-on cost $28/month total; new Business plan at $24/month is cheaper but AI now included by default
  - Existing users who purchased AI add-on before May 2025 retain access to those features

Notion AI features are now included only in Business and Enterprise plans, no longer available as a separate add-on.

input.subquestion: What are Confluence's exact pricing tiers and costs?
input.rationale: This page covers all Confluence plans and costs in detail, recently updated.

data.subquestion: What are Confluence's exact pricing tiers and costs?
data.rationale: This page covers all Confluence plans and costs in detail, recently updated.

data.summary:
Read https://www.featurebase.app/blog/confluence-pricing:
Confluence Cloud offers four tiers: Free ($0 for up to 10 users), Standard ($680/year or $5.42/user/month for 1–10 users), Premium ($1,300/year or $10.44/user/month for 1–10 users), and Enterprise (custom pricing for 801+ users). Confluence Data Center pricing starts at ~$27,000/year for 500 users and ~$54,000/year for 1,000 users. Real-world costs typically range from $1,000–$5,000/year for small teams, scaling to $75,000+ for large teams when including add-ons and extra features.
Key facts:
  - Confluence Cloud Free: $0 for up to 10 users
  - Confluence Cloud Standard: $680/year ($5.42/user/month) for 1–10 users
  - Confluence Cloud Premium: $1,300/year ($10.44/user/month) for 1–10 users
  - Confluence Cloud Enterprise: Custom pricing for 801+ users
  - Confluence Data Center: ~$27,000/year for 500 users; ~$54,000/year for 1,000 users
  - Real-world costs typically $1,000–$5,000/year for small teams; up to $75,000+ for 500-user teams when including add-ons

Confluence Data Center pricing starts at ~$27,000/year for 500 users and ~$54,000/year for 1,000 users. Real-world costs typically range from $1,000–$5,000/year for small teams, scaling to $75,000+ for large teams when including add-ons and extra features.

input.subquestion: What are Coda's pricing tiers and costs?
input.rationale: Detailed 2025 Coda pricing breakdown from a credible analysis blog.

data.subquestion: What are Coda's pricing tiers and costs?
data.rationale: Detailed 2025 Coda pricing breakdown from a credible analysis blog.

data.summary:
Read https://www.eesel.ai/blog/coda-pricing:
Coda offers four pricing tiers: Free ($0), Pro ($10/Doc Maker/month or $12/month), Team ($30/Doc Maker/month or $36/month annually), and Enterprise (custom pricing). The billing model is unique: costs are based solely on the number of 'Doc Makers' (users who can create and structurally modify docs), while unlimited Editors and Viewers can be added for free. Key differences include doc size limits, version history retention (7 days free, 30 days Pro, unlimited Team/Enterprise), and support levels.
Key facts:
  - Free plan: $0, limited doc size, 7-day version history, community support
  - Pro plan: $10/Doc Maker/month (or $12/month), large doc size, 30-day version history, email support
  - Team plan: $30/Doc Maker/month (or $36/month annually), very large doc size, unlimited version history, dedicated support
  - Enterprise plan: custom pricing, unlimited doc size, audit logs, dedicated success manager
  - Billing based solely on number of Doc Makers; Editors and Viewers are free
  - Doc Makers have the power to create docs and change their structure; Editors can modify content within existing structures; Viewers can view and comment

The billing model is unique: costs are based solely on the number of 'Doc Makers' (users who can create and structurally modify docs), while unlimited Editors and Viewers can be added for free. Key differences include doc size limits, version history retention (7 days free, 30 days Pro, unlimited Team/Enterprise), and support levels.

Pro plan: $10/Doc Maker/month (or $12/month), large doc size, 30-day version history, email support
Team plan: $30/Doc Maker/month (or $36/month annually), very large doc size, unlimited version history, dedicated support
Enterprise plan: custom pricing, unlimited doc size, audit logs, dedicated success manager
Billing based solely on number of Doc Makers; Editors and Viewers are free
Doc Makers have the power to create docs and change their structure; Editors can modify content within existing structures; Viewers can view and comment

data.subquestion: What are ClickUp's exact pricing tiers and feature differences?
data.rationale: Plaky has proven reliable for pricing details; this article covers ClickUp pricing tiers in depth.

data.summary:
Read https://plaky.com/learn/plaky/clickup-pricing/:
ClickUp offers 4 pricing tiers: Free ($0), Unlimited ($7/user/month annually or $10/month monthly), Business ($12/user/month annually or $19/month monthly), and Enterprise (custom quote). The Free plan includes unlimited tasks, users, and custom views but is limited to 5 Spaces, 60 MB storage, and 1-day activity log. Paid plans unlock features like unlimited storage, Gantt charts, automations, and advanced reporting. An optional AI add-on costs $7/user/month on paid plans only.
Key facts:
  - Free plan: $0, unlimited users/tasks/views, limited to 5 Spaces and 60 MB storage
  - Unlimited plan: $7/user/month (annual) or $10/month (monthly), includes unlimited storage and Gantt charts
  - Business plan: $12/user/month (annual) or $19/month (monthly), adds automations, advanced reporting, and 2FA
  - Enterprise plan: Custom pricing (POA), includes white labeling, unlimited automations (250,000), priority support, and HIPAA compliance
  - ClickUp AI add-on: $7/user/month on paid plans only, includes AI Knowledge Manager, Project Manager, and Writer for Work
  - Free plan limitations: 40 lists/100 folders per space, 60 custom field uses, 1-day activity log, no AI access

The Free plan includes unlimited tasks, users, and custom views but is limited to 5 Spaces, 60 MB storage, and 1-day activity log. Paid plans unlock features like unlimited storage, Gantt charts, automations, and advanced reporting. An optional AI add-on costs $7/user/month on paid plans only.

data.chosen_subquestion: What are the key differentiators of Confluence, ClickUp, and Coda vs. Notion?

input.subquestion: What are the key differentiators of Confluence, ClickUp, and Coda vs. Notion?
input.rationale: This recently updated article covers top Notion competitors with feature and positioning comparisons.

data.subquestion: What are the key differentiators of Confluence, ClickUp, and Coda vs. Notion?
data.rationale: This recently updated article covers top Notion competitors with feature and positioning comparisons.

data.summary:
Read https://www.techbloat.com/top-8-notion-alternatives-and-competitors.html:
The page provides detailed comparisons of ClickUp, Coda, and Confluence against Notion across multiple dimensions. ClickUp is described as more project-management focused with stronger support for dependencies, workload views, sprints, time tracking, and task management compared to Notion. Coda is positioned as better for teams needing powerful tables, buttons, formulas, and app-like workflows, particularly for product roadmaps and approval flows. Confluence is characterized as better for larger teams needing formal knowledge bases, governance, permissions, and Jira integration, with less flexibility as a personal productivity tool.
Key facts:
  - ClickUp offers stronger native support for dependencies, workload views, sprints, time tracking, and recurring tasks versus Notion's lighter project management
  - Coda combines documents with programmable tables, buttons, and formulas; useful for product roadmaps, approval flows, and connecting to Slack, Gmail, Jira, and Google Calendar
  - Confluence offers mature permissions, page history, templates, comments, and enterprise administration; particularly popular with engineering, product, IT, and operations teams using Jira
  - ClickUp connects documentation directly to execution, making it stronger for project execution than Notion
  - Confluence is less flexible as a personal productivity tool but better for governed documentation and larger teams
  - Coda feels like a blend of docs, spreadsheets, and lightweight applications, designed for custom workflows and internal tools

Coda is positioned as better for teams needing powerful tables, buttons, formulas, and app-like workflows, particularly for product roadmaps and approval flows. Confluence is characterized as better for larger teams needing formal knowledge bases, governance, permissions, and Jira integration, with less flexibility as a personal productivity tool.

Coda combines documents with programmable tables, buttons, and formulas; useful for product roadmaps, approval flows, and connecting to Slack, Gmail, Jira, and Google Calendar
Confluence offers mature permissions, page history, templates, comments, and enterprise administration; particularly popular with engineering, product, IT, and operations teams using Jira

data.evaluation: I now have all the information needed for a comprehensive competitive brief. Let me compile it.

data.answer:
# Competitive Brief: Notion — Top 3 Competitors & Pricing

---

## Notion (Baseline)

| Plan | Monthly Billing | Annual Billing |
|---|---|---|
| Free | $0 | $0 |
| Plus | $12/user/mo | $10/user/mo |
| Business | $24/user/mo | $20/user/mo |
| Enterprise | Custom (POA) | Custom (POA) |

**Key notes:** As of May 2025, Notion removed the standalone AI add-on. Notion AI is now bundled exclusively into the Business and Enterprise tiers. Plus users get limited trial access only [1].

---

## Competitor #1 — Confluence (Atlassian)

**Why it's a top competitor:** Confluence is the most established wiki/knowledge-base tool in the enterprise market. It targets engineering, product, IT, and operations teams — especially those already in the Atlassian ecosystem (Jira). It competes with Notion on team documentation and knowledge management, but is less flexible for personal productivity [6].

### Pricing (Cloud)

| Plan | Annual Billing |
|---|---|
| Free | $0 (up to 10 users) |
| Standard | ~$5.42/user/mo ($680/yr for 1–10 users) |
| Premium | ~$10.44/user/mo ($1,300/yr for 1–10 users) |
| Enterprise | Custom (801+ users) |

**Key notes:**
- Costs scale with user count in tiers — pricing per user actually **decreases** as team size grows.
- A **Data Center** (self-hosted) option starts at ~$27,000/year for 500 users [2].
- Real-world costs for small teams run $1,000–$5,000/year; large teams (500+) can exceed $75,000/year with add-ons [2].
- Confluence's Atlassian ecosystem lock-in is a major differentiator — deep Jira integration is a key reason enterprises choose it over Notion.

### Confluence vs. Notion
| Factor | Confluence | Notion |
|---|---|---|
| Best for | Enterprise knowledge bases, eng/product teams | Flexible all-in-one workspace, SMBs |
| AI | Included on paid plans | Business/Enterprise only |
| Governance | Mature permissions, audit logs | More limited on lower tiers |
| Flexibility | Lower (structured wiki) | Higher (docs, DBs, tasks) |

---

## Competitor #2 — ClickUp

**Why it's a top competitor:** ClickUp is the most direct feature-for-feature rival to Notion, competing on docs, databases, and task management simultaneously. It differentiates by offering stronger native project management features: dependencies, Gantt charts, sprints, time tracking, and workload views — areas where Notion is comparatively lightweight [6].

### Pricing

| Plan | Annual Billing | Monthly Billing |
|---|---|---|
| Free | $0 (unlimited users) | $0 |
| Unlimited | $7/user/mo | $10/user/mo |
| Business | $12/user/mo | $19/user/mo |
| Enterprise | Custom (POA) | Custom (POA) |
| AI Add-on | +$7/user/mo | +$7/user/mo |

**Key notes:**
- The Free plan is notably generous: unlimited users, tasks, and custom views (limited to 5 Spaces and 60 MB storage) [3].
- AI (ClickUp Brain) is an **optional add-on** at $7/user/mo on paid plans only, covering AI Knowledge Manager, Project Manager, and Writer for Work [3].
- Business tier unlocks automations, advanced reporting, and 2FA.
- Enterprise adds white labeling, 250,000 automations/mo, and HIPAA compliance.

### ClickUp vs. Notion
| Factor | ClickUp | Notion |
|---|---|---|
| Best for | Project execution + documentation | Flexible knowledge/docs/DBs |
| AI | Optional add-on ($7/user/mo) | Bundled in Business+ |
| Project mgmt | Native (Gantt, sprints, time tracking) | Lightweight / workaround-heavy |
| Learning curve | Steeper | Moderate |

---

## Competitor #3 — Coda

**Why it's a top competitor:** Coda blends documents, spreadsheets, and lightweight application logic into a single surface — the closest architectural rival to Notion's "all-in-one" premise. It stands out for its powerful tables, buttons, formulas, and workflow automation, making it especially strong for product teams building internal tools like roadmaps and approval flows [6].

### Pricing

| Plan | Per Doc Maker/mo (Annual) | Per Doc Maker/mo (Monthly) |
|---|---|---|
| Free | $0 | $0 |
| Pro | $10 | $12 |
| Team | $30 | $36 |
| Enterprise | Custom (POA) | Custom (POA) |

**Key notes:**
- Coda's billing model is **unique**: pricing is based only on **"Doc Makers"** (users who create/structurally modify docs). Unlimited **Editors and Viewers are free** on all plans [4].
- This makes Coda highly cost-effective for large teams with few power users and many consumers/collaborators.
- Pro adds 30-day version history and larger doc sizes; Team adds unlimited automation, doc locking, private folders, and priority support [4].

### Coda vs. Notion
| Factor | Coda | Notion |
|---|---|---|
| Best for | App-like workflows, power users building internal tools | Broad team knowledge & notes |
| Billing model | Doc Makers only (viewers free) | Per seat (all editors) |
| Programmability | High (buttons, formulas, automation) | Moderate (limited formulas) |
| Learning curve | Moderate–High | Moderate |

---

## Summary Comparison Table

| Tool | Free Tier | Entry Paid | Mid Tier | AI Included? |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Notion** | ✅ | $10/user/mo *(annual)* | $20/user/mo *(annual)* | Business+ only |
| **Confluence** | ✅ (10 users) | ~$5.42/user/mo | ~$10.44/user/mo | Paid plans |
| **ClickUp** | ✅ (unlimited users) | $7/user/mo | $12/user/mo | Add-on (+$7/user) |
| **Coda** | ✅ | $10/Doc Maker/mo | $30/Doc Maker/mo | Enterprise/add-on |

---

**Bottom line:** Confluence wins on enterprise governance and Jira integration. ClickUp wins on project execution depth. Coda wins on programmable doc/app workflows with its viewer-free pricing model. Notion competes across all three battlefields with a more unified, flexible UX — but at a higher per-seat cost for AI features.
